notes:
đóng usually means to close / shut something. You use it for things like doors, windows, eyes, lids—anything that can be physically shut.

It’s followed directly by the object: no preposition needed.  
Structure: đóng + cửa / mắt / nắp (i.e. đóng cửa)

It also very commonly means to pay (money) or to contribute (like fees, money to a fund).  
Structure: đóng + tiền / học phí / thuế (i.e. đóng học phí)

Other everyday meanings: to play a role (đóng vai) and to nail something (đóng đinh). Opposite of “đóng” (close) is mở — super useful pair to remember.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you close the window for me?
Remember to close the door when you leave the room.
I have to close the computer before going to sleep every night.
exampleSentences: Anh có thể đóng cửa sổ giúp em được không?
Chị nhớ đóng cửa khi ra khỏi phòng nhé.
Em phải đóng máy tính trước khi đi ngủ mỗi tối.
notes_md:
**đóng** usually means **to close / shut** something. You use it for things like doors, windows, eyes, lids—anything that can be physically shut.

It’s followed directly by the object: no preposition needed.  
`Structure: đóng + cửa / mắt / nắp (i.e. đóng cửa)`

It also very commonly means **to pay (money)** or **to contribute** (like fees, money to a fund).  
`Structure: đóng + tiền / học phí / thuế (i.e. đóng học phí)`

Other everyday meanings: **to play a role** (đóng vai) and **to nail** something (đóng đinh). Opposite of “đóng” (close) is **mở** — super useful pair to remember.

example.es: Por favor, cierra la puerta cuando salgas.
example.en: Please close the door when you go out.
example.vi: Hãy đóng cửa khi ra ngoài.

definitions.vi: Để đóng hoặc khép lại một cái gì đó.
